notes:
Opening/plaintiff work notes 2026-06-30T06:04Z

Objective: opening statement for proposition: by 2026-05-08 11:59 PM ET, a message/note credibly reported to have been written by Jeffrey Epstein and intended as a suicide note/final message/equivalent communication had been made publicly available. Standard: preponderance.

Record inspected: get_case/list_evidence showed one juror-visible case packet: ev_90fa205907f7_market-page, market-page.txt, sha256 90fa205907f7af6483463b7dcda6e06a38a2e22fbe19312319fdf46a78f14646, 4641 bytes. Read full text via read_evidence_range. Packet preserves Polymarket rule/market metadata. Rule: YES if any message/note written by Jeffrey Epstein, intended as suicide note/final message/equivalent, publicly available by listed date; qualifying note must be credibly reported as written by Epstein and intended as such; public availability may be official, leaked, or otherwise disclosed; resolution source is consensus of credible reporting. May 8 market metadata: market id 2128535, slug epstein-suicide-note-released-by-may-8, outcomes Yes/No, captured prices 0.0005/0.9995, active true, closed false, UMA disputed, page-visible final review history proposed Yes/No. Packet says Polymarket event context captured 2026-05-12 stated a U.S. District Judge in New York unsealed a purported suicide note attributed to Epstein on 2026-05-07, while noting authenticity/intent doubts; packet cautions this is market metadata, not independent proof.

Source map: decisive elements are public availability by May 8, message/note, credible reporting as written by Epstein, intended as suicide/final/equivalent, and effect of authenticity/intent caveats. Strongest primary targets for later admission: SDNY docket/order in United States v. Nicholas Tartaglione, 16-cr-832 (KMK), especially order dated 2026-05-06 and docketed copy/original material ordered unsealed; CourtListener possible RECAP copy. Credible reporting targets: AP/PBS, ABC, WSJ, TIME, Guardian, Axios, CBS/local sources with note image/text. Adverse targets: reports that authorship was unclear/unverified; court order language 'purportedly authored'; government/DOJ caveats; Mark Epstein authenticity denial if admissible.

Investigation performed: Web searches for 'Epstein suicide note released by May 8', 'purported suicide note Jeffrey Epstein May 7 2026', 'Epstein suicide note May 7 2026 judge unsealed', 'Watcha want me to do Epstein', 'Nicholas Tartaglione Epstein suicide note docket', 'Epstein suicide note Kenneth Karas Tartaglione', and focused NYSD/CourtListener searches. Opened SDNY order PDF at https://www.nysd.uscourts.gov/sites/default/files/2026-05/16cr832%20-%20Tartaglione%20Order%20-%2026.5.6.pdf (not admitted yet). Key observed contents: order grants NYT request to unseal alleged 'suicide note purportedly authored by Jeffrey Epstein, recovered by ... Tartaglione, and then reportedly submitted by ... Tartaglione's attorneys'; directs clerk to docket a copy of material placed in vault on 2021-05-20 and unseal original; dated 2026-05-06. This is a source target, not current evidence. Search did not yet locate actual note PDF/docket attachment; later phase should try CourtListener RECAP docket entries around 610-613, SDNY docket, article image downloads, browser/curl image URLs.

Reasoning/draft theory: record alone proves market framing and the exact factual standard, not the underlying court-note facts. It does show the dispute is not whether Polymarket/UMA was right but whether the factual condition occurred. Opening should be candid: current admitted packet is enough to frame burden and source targets; later evidence should provide court order/note/reporting. Likely plaintiff theory: credible reporting and official unsealing before May 8 satisfy the proposition under preponderance even if note remains only 'purported' and not forensically authenticated, because the rule asks for credible reporting, not certainty. Major adverse caveat: if defendant proves no credible reporting as written by Epstein or no suicide/final intent, or only market chatter, plaintiff loses. Unresolved gaps preserved in /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Defendant opening turn notes. Opportunity openings:defendant, action allowed record_opening_statement only. Inspected current record via get_case/list_evidence/stat_evidence/read_evidence_range. Visible evidence only ev_90fa205907f7_market-page, sha256 90fa205907f7af6483463b7dcda6e06a38a2e22fbe19312319fdf46a78f14646, text/plain 4641 bytes, case_packet. Full packet read. Packet proves market/rule framing, market identifiers/status, and Polymarket metadata statement about a NY federal judge unsealing a purported note on May 7 with doubts. It does not prove underlying court document, public availability, authorship, intent, credible reporting, or custody. Plaintiff opening concedes need for primary court/source chain. Source map/searches before opening: searched web queries for Epstein suicide note May 7 2026 Tartaglione, purported suicide note Jeffrey Epstein May 7 unsealed, Jeffrey Epstein suicide note Tartaglione unsealed; then official/docket queries site:storage.courtlistener.com Tartaglione Epstein suicide note PDF, Tartaglione Epstein suicide note Karas pdf, United States v. Tartaglione Epstein suicide note. Leads found for later evidence-admitting phase: SDNY official May 6 2026 order PDF at nysd.uscourts.gov/sites/default/files/2026-05/16cr832%20-%20Tartaglione%20Order%20-%2026.5.6.pdf; Courthouse News NYT unsealing letter PDF; AP, CBS, Courthouse News, Fox, Washington Post, NYMag/Intelligencer reports; adverse Business Insider/Mark Epstein forgery claim. These are unadmitted and should not be cited as record proof in opening. Defense theory: plaintiff bears burden with admitted source chain, not market metadata; key weight problems are purported/alleged wording, unsigned/unauthenticated note, Tartaglione provenance/conflict, lack of official DOJ/FBI authentication or possible absence from BOP/DOJ records, possible forgery challenge. If later admitted sources prove public release and credible reporting, plaintiff may meet standard because rule requires credible reporting, not forensic certainty; opening should preserve that current record does not yet do so. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md with source URLs, queries, gaps, and phase decision.

Arguments/plaintiff work notes 2026-06-30

Re-scanned current record with get_case/list_evidence/stat/read_evidence_range. No new record evidence before plaintiff submissions except original case packet ev_90fa205907f7_market-page and the two openings. Defendant opening emphasized three links: public availability by deadline; credible reporting that writing was Epstein's; credible reporting/content showing suicide/final/equivalent. This is the correct battlefield.

Evidence plan: admit primary court source and credible public reporting before relying on them. Primary: official SDNY order in U.S. v. Tartaglione, 16-cr-832 (KMK). Downloaded PDF with curl from official nysd.uscourts.gov URL to /tmp/tartaglione-order.pdf. Observed sha256 7225441cb802084e2b9485ebc2c6d246fbfa08e109fe650cb14f445dc4745ca0; size 128,992 bytes. pdftotext not installed; used web-extracted official PDF text and attorney-created relevant extract rather than full binary. Submitted accepted evidence: ev_87eda7a5f14d_submitted-evidence-01-plaintiff-87eda7a5f14d, sha256 87eda7a5f14da6f6492443d9e3e63d5a5cbb1e7d75ae0a49c7f6d7f1cb254511. It shows May 6, 2026 order granting NYT request as to the Note; describes alleged 'suicide note purportedly authored by Jeffrey Epstein, recovered by Tartaglione, and reportedly submitted by Tartaglione's attorneys'; directs clerk to docket copy and unseal original material. Weight: strong on public unsealing mechanism/custody path; caveated on authorship.

Reporting source: PBS NewsHour/AP article by Michael R. Sisak and Larry Neumeister. Downloaded HTML with curl; observed sha256 7bc6354f83080c555ecb4ab0c17eb26bde83c6a1830ad012aed37e0122008e3b, size 246,650 bytes. Extracted relevant text with Python html.parser; extracted body 6,959 bytes. Submitted accepted evidence: ev_a2ccbb719d1f_submitted-evidence-02-plaintiff-a2ccbb719d1f, sha256 a2ccbb719d1fab06be2d4b91c78b80fbaeef2c1c58aa8920fb6856caaa98bf13. It shows publication May 6 8:01 PM EDT, update May 7 5:36 PM EDT; says the note was made public Wednesday; says Judge Karas ordered release; gives Tartaglione claim; gives note content including 'time to say goodbye', 'NO FUN', 'NOT WORTH IT'; includes image caption that note was purported to be written by Epstein and released by SDNY on May 6, 2026; preserves adverse caveat that it is unclear who wrote note and it was not in DOJ reports/recent release.

Evidence analysis: ev_90fa proves rule/May 8 market frame and market metadata but not independent underlying fact. ev_87eda proves official order/unsealing process and court-described provenance/caveats. ev_a2cc proves credible AP/PBS public reporting before deadline and supplies note content/intent context plus adverse caveats. Combined, preponderance is satisfied because the rule asks for 'credibly reported to have been written' and public availability, not conclusive handwriting authentication. Intent/equivalent communication is supported by context (after suspected suicide attempt) and text ('choose one's time to say goodbye', 'NO FUN', 'NOT WORTH IT'), despite caveats.

Adverse/missing checks: Actual note image/document not admitted; search did not find docket attachment within time. Official order and AP/PBS extract are enough for merits but later rebuttal could pursue CourtListener/RECAP entries around docket 610-613 or article image URLs if needed. Mark Epstein denial/authenticity concerns not admitted; AP/PBS caveat handles the central adverse point. Filing will avoid overclaiming authentication and argue credible reporting/preponderance.

Defendant merits turn notes. Opportunity arguments:defendant; final action submit_argument; evidence submission allowed. At start of turn, inspected current prompt/record and scanned evidence list. New plaintiff evidence: ev_87eda7a5f14d_submitted-evidence-01-plaintiff-87eda7a5f14d (SDNY order extract, 3043 bytes, sha256 87eda7a5f14da6f6492443d9e3e63d5a5cbb1e7d75ae0a49c7f6d7f1cb254511) and ev_a2ccbb719d1f_submitted-evidence-02-plaintiff-a2ccbb719d1f (PBS/AP report extract, 2579 bytes, sha256 a2ccbb719d1fab06be2d4b91c78b80fbaeef2c1c58aa8920fb6856caaa98bf13). Read full bytes of both. PX-2 establishes court order/release mechanism before May 8 but is a relevant-text extract and repeatedly uses alleged/purported/recovered by Tartaglione/reportedly submitted; it does not authenticate handwriting or intent. PX-3 establishes AP/PBS publication before deadline and note text/circumstances, but expressly says unclear who wrote it and note absent from lengthy government reports and recent DOJ file release. Searched adverse/authenticity sources: Mark Epstein suicide note forgery Business Insider May 2026; AP handwriting experts newly released Epstein note matches May 8 2026; Jeffrey Epstein brother suicide note forgery Little Rascals. Found Business Insider article by Jacob Shamsian and submitted relevant extract as ev_07b7be066555_submitted-evidence-03-defendant-07b7be066555, sha256 07b7be066555a0766c37ccbbcf15609c676a715461493dbe8752b25128e635c9, 3705 bytes. It reports Mark Epstein forgery challenge, Tartaglione's criminal/provenance issues, absence from DOJ/IG materials, and also counterweights that note was sealed in May 2021 before public release of similar phrase emails and that multiple people tried to authenticate it in 2019/2020. Adverse-to-defense lead found but not submitted by defense: AP later story says handwriting experts found the newly released note matched another note found after Epstein's death; experts reportedly could not definitively identify Epstein due to lack of authenticated samples. Merits theory: concede plaintiff now has stronger deadline/public availability proof; argue remaining rule element is not merely report of a claim but credible report of authorship and suicide/final intent. Court unsealing is access, not factual authentication. AP caveat and D-1 forgery/provenance conflict keep plaintiff below preponderance on authorship/intent. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Rebuttal/plaintiff work notes 2026-06-30

Re-scanned evidence list. Defendant added D-1, ev_07b7be066555_submitted-evidence-03-defendant-07b7be066555, Business Insider report on Mark Epstein forgery challenge and note provenance. stat/read_evidence_range read full 3,705 bytes.

Opponent's strongest point: defense says publication is shown but credible authorship/intent are not; relies on Mark Epstein saying forgery, Tartaglione provenance, absence from DOJ/IG files, and court/reporting caveats.

Analysis of D-1: Helpful to defense: Mark Epstein says note is forgery; says a professional forger could copy voice from public emails; doubts Tartaglione had/found the note; says no suicide/no suicide note. Helpful to plaintiff/counterweights in same exhibit: BI reports note was sealed in May 2021, before Epstein emails with the Little Rascals reference became public in 2026; timeline reportedly shows the note passed through multiple people who tried to authenticate it in 2019/2020; Tartaglione described note in a podcast before Epstein files release; IG report ultimately characterized July incident as a first suicide attempt; note content includes goodbye language and phrases reported as used by Epstein in communications. D-1 therefore does not defeat credible reporting; it confirms dispute and provides an alternate family-member belief.

No additional evidence submitted. Reason: D-1 itself contains the relevant rebuttal counterweights; plaintiff record already has SDNY order and AP/PBS report. Rebuttal should stay narrow: rule asks credible reporting, not official authentication or family-member agreement; a single family-member denial, especially one tied to his broader no-suicide view per D-1, does not outweigh court-described provenance plus AP/PBS public reporting before deadline. Remaining gap: actual note image not admitted, but not essential to respond to D-1 because the proposition's standard is credible reporting and D-1 does not erase that.

Defendant surrebuttal notes. Opportunity surrebuttals:defendant; final actions submit_surrebuttal or pass. Inspected full case via get_case, list_evidence, and stat_evidence for D-1. No new submitted plaintiff evidence in rebuttal; plaintiff re-offered PX-2/PX-3 as rebuttal exhibits and used D-1's internal counterweights. Plaintiff rebuttal says rule does not require DOJ/court/family/forensic certainty; D-1 is one interested family member's view; Mark Epstein's murder/no-suicide position explains his rejection; D-1's sealed-before-email-publication fact undercuts the Little Rascals public-email forgery theory; D-1 also reports timeline/authentication attempts and IG first-suicide-attempt characterization. Surrebuttal plan: accept lower standard but argue credible reporting must be of authorship, not merely credible reporting that a disputed claim exists. D-1 counterweights do not become plaintiff proof: sealed-before-public-emails defeats one possible copying mechanism but not provenance/authorship doubts; authentication attempts are not authentication results; Tartaglione's pre-release statements remain self-serving and from same compromised source. Focus admitted record: no note image, no handwriting comparison, no authenticated sample, no official investigative use, AP says unclear who wrote it, DOJ/IG absence remains material. No new evidence submitted because record already supplies the narrow surrebuttal points. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Closing/plaintiff work notes 2026-06-30

Record scan: get_case/list_evidence/stat_evidence performed. Visible evidence unchanged: ev_90fa205907f7_market-page; ev_87eda7a5f14d plaintiff SDNY order extract; ev_a2ccbb719d1f plaintiff AP/PBS extract; ev_07b7be066555 defendant Business Insider extract. No evidence may be added in closing.

Defendant surrebuttal position: public release is strong, but authorship/intent remain only caveated; asks for note image, handwriting comparison, authenticated sample, or official investigative use. Says attempts to authenticate are not results and public 2026 email issue is only one forgery theory.

Closing theory: answer with exact proposition/rule: credible reporting, not certainty. Acknowledge record lacks original note image and forensic authentication. Emphasize decisive facts: (1) public availability before deadline shown by May 6 court order and May 6 AP/PBS publication/release; (2) credible reporting shown by AP/PBS and court-described provenance, with court-vault/docketing/unsealing and no prosecutor opposition; (3) intent/equivalent shown by suspected suicide-attempt context and note language about choosing time to say goodbye/NO FUN/NOT WORTH IT, plus D-1's IG characterization of July incident as first suicide attempt; (4) defense caveats do not defeat preponderance because D-1's Mark Epstein forgery theory is a family-member view and contains counterweights: note sealed in 2021 before public 2026 emails, attempted authentication in 2019/2020, Tartaglione described note before Epstein files release. Weakest point is lack of original note image; closing should frame it as not required by rule's standard.

Defendant closing notes. Opportunity closings:defendant; final action deliver_closing_statement only; no evidence submission or technical reports. Inspected current record and evidence list with get_case/list_evidence in closing prompt context. Evidence unchanged: PX-1 market packet, PX-2 SDNY order extract, PX-3 AP/PBS extract, D-1 Business Insider/Mark Epstein extract. Plaintiff closing concedes key defense limits: no original note image, no completed handwriting finding, PX-3 says unclear who wrote it, D-1 reports Mark Epstein forgery view and absence from DOJ/IG materials. Plaintiff relies on lower 'credibly reported' standard. Closing plan: concede public availability before May 8 is established; argue proposition still requires credible reporting of authorship and suicide/final/equivalent intent, not merely credible reporting that a disputed document and Tartaglione claim exist. Court order is access/provenance, not authentication; AP/PBS caveat is central; D-1 adds close-family challenge and official-file absence; D-1 counterweights do not cure gap because attempted authentication is not successful authentication and sealed-before-emails only defeats one forgery mechanism. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.
